page: Microsoft Azure EC2as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECasv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware. However, it is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance persistent storage volumes as needed. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the EC2as_v6 provides a memory-optimized profile suitable for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute capacity. This resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for small-scale database systems, in-memory caching, and memory-sensitive enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure EC2as_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ECasv6 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but it is Premium Storage capable, enabling the attachment of external high-performance storage. Designed with a memory-optimized resource profile, this server is configured for workloads that demand a larger memory capacity relative to processing power. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource sharing, while the lack of bundled local storage allows users to customize their storage architecture using external disks. It is suitable for small-scale databases, in-memory caching, and memory-sensitive enterprise applications.
